services.title: Design and development that sells.
services.lede: Design, development, and visual content — all in one seamless process.
services.01.title: Brand identity & graphic design
services.01.text: Logo, color palette, and a complete visual system. A brand that's instantly recognizable and feels cohesive across every touchpoint.
services.02.title: Web design & development
services.02.text: Fast, custom-built websites — designed and coded by the same person. No templates, no handoffs. PageSpeed 97 on real client projects.
services.03.title: Content & social media design

why.title:
The difference isn't price.
It's speed.
why.lede: An agency puts three people between your idea and the result: the salesperson, the project manager, and the person who actually does the work. Every layer adds time and dilutes what you asked for. With me, there are no layers — you talk directly to the person designing and building your project.
why.them.01: You talk to a salesperson; the work is done by someone you never meet
why.them.02: Your brief passes through 3 people before it becomes actual work
why.them.03: Revisions and changes take days to coordinate
why.them.04: Design and code are handled by different teams — things get lost in handoff
why.them.05: You're also paying for the overhead: office, managers, commissions
why.me.01: You talk directly to the person designing and building your project
why.me.02: What you ask for arrives without anyone diluting it in the middle
why.me.03: A change gets resolved the same day, not next week
why.me.04: Design and code are done by the same person — nothing gets lost
why.me.05: You pay for the work, not the structure around it
why.close: It's not that agencies are bad. It's that for most projects, that structure is overhead you pay for and time you wait through — without getting a better result.
focus.title:
Clear thinking.
Real results.
focus.lede:
Design. Development. Content.
All in one person.
process.title: From brief to delivery.
process.01.title: We talk
process.01.text: A 20-minute call or a short form. You tell me what you need, who your customer is, and what you want to achieve. No decks, no endless meetings — that's enough to know if we're a good fit.
process.02.title: Proposal in 48 h
process.02.text: Within two days you receive a document with the exact scope: what's included, what isn't, delivery timeline, and price. We review it together and adjust anything before signing.
process.03.title: Production with visibility
process.03.text: I don't disappear for two weeks and show up with something finished. I work in partial deliveries so you can see real progress, give feedback in time, and the final result is never a surprise.
process.04.title: Ready to use
process.04.text: Organized files, clean code, and everything you need to take the project from there. No dependency on me. And if you need a tweak in the first 7 days, we handle it at no extra cost.

The person
behind the work.
about.bio.01: If you're looking for someone who can design, build and understand the strategy behind it — without bouncing you between teams or asking you to translate what you need — you're in the right place.
about.bio.02: For 2+ years I've managed everything digital at Orbidental: e-commerce, Meta and Google Ads, product photography, and design. Not theory — real environments, real metrics.

testimonials.title:
What they say
about my work.
testimonials.01.text: Alexander gave us a visual identity far more powerful than we expected. He captured exactly the essence of fire and set us completely apart from other food brands. People recognize us instantly now — the design is unique and speaks for itself.
testimonials.01.name: Felipe Romero
testimonials.02.text: The result exceeded our expectations. Alexander understood exactly what we wanted: the feel of a jungle, but unmistakably a nightclub. Every piece reflects the name and energy of the brand.
testimonials.02.name: Mayra Castaño
testimonials.03.text: We couldn't be happier with the result. We wanted something urban, with that graffiti edge but applied to streetwear — and Alexander got it from the very first conversation. The identity came out exactly as we imagined it.
testimonials.03.name: Steven Mesa
trusted.label:
Brands that
have trusted me
faq.title:
Things people
always ask.
faq.lede: Clear answers so you can make confident decisions and we can work with full transparency.
faq.01.q: Do you work with clients outside Colombia?
faq.01.a: Yes — I work 100% remotely and have clients in multiple countries. We coordinate via WhatsApp, Zoom, or whatever channel you prefer. Time zones haven't been an issue. I'm in Bogotá (UTC-5) and I adapt to my clients' schedules.
faq.02.a: I work with a 50% deposit to kick things off and the remaining 50% on delivery. For larger projects we split it into milestones. For international clients I accept PayPal or Wise.
faq.03.a: It depends on the service, but generally I include two rounds of revisions within the agreed scope. If you need more after that, we price it separately — no surprises. What matters is that the final result is right.
faq.04.q: What time zone do you work in?
faq.04.a: I'm in Bogotá, Colombia — UTC-5, no daylight saving. I'm usually available between 8am and 8pm Colombian time, but I'm flexible when we need to schedule a call across time zones.
faq.05.a: Always. Before starting any project we sign an agreement that defines the scope, timeline, price, and delivery terms. It protects both of us and prevents misunderstandings down the road.
faq.06.q: What if I need changes after delivery?
faq.06.a: Minor changes within the original scope are covered at no extra cost if you request them within the first 7 days. After that, or for significant direction changes, we price them separately — always with a clear quote before touching anything.

tw.hero.sub: Every brand on this page was losing something — sales, recognition, trust — before I came in. Below, what happened after.
tw.stat1.label: PageSpeed, Orbidental
tw.stat2.label: products in a B2B catalog
tw.stat3.label: middlemen between you and the code
tw.orbidental.lead: A dental supply distributor sizes up their vendor in seconds. If the site loads slow and breaks on mobile, they assume the whole operation is like that — and they leave for a competitor.
tw.orbidental.metric2: SEO or content lost in migration
tw.orbidental.detail: I migrated a heavy Divi site to clean code, preserving all existing rankings. Now I'm connecting the catalog to their inventory system via API — code-level integration, not a plugin.
tw.pulse.problem: The challenge: prove I don't repeat a single style. Fictional brand, 100% real code and deployment.
tw.pulse.solution: The call: different language, different palette, different typeface — no frameworks, no dependencies. Vanilla, live.
tw.acelerarte.problem: The challenge: motorcycle racing is intense to live, but its content almost always looks generic.
tw.acelerarte.solution: The call: my own motorsport flyer brand — competition-grade aesthetics that recognized riders actually repost.
tw.fueggo.problem: The challenge: the brand looked different at the restaurant, on packaging, and on uniforms — nothing tied together.
tw.fueggo.solution: The call: a complete system — logo, palette, typography — applied to real, print-ready pieces.
tw.jungla.problem: The challenge: an intense nightlife concept — masked DJs, jungle aesthetic — with no identity to match.
tw.jungla.solution: The call: a system that hits just as hard on a flyer, a facade, and a uniform.
tw.rebel.problem: The challenge: in a saturated streetwear market, you're either instantly recognizable or invisible.
tw.rebel.solution: The call: a complete system, from logo to packaging, built so every piece is unmistakable.
tw.cap.title: Every project goes through one person
tw.cap.sub: You don't coordinate a designer, a developer, and an SEO specialist. You talk to me and the project moves forward without friction between phases. The cases above are a selection — this is the full scope.
tw.cap.01.title: Brand design
tw.cap.01.desc: Complete visual identity: logo, palette, typography, and brand guidelines.
tw.cap.02.title: Frontend development
tw.cap.02.desc: Clean code, no unnecessary frameworks. Fast to load, easy to maintain.
tw.cap.03.title: Technical SEO
tw.cap.03.desc: Structure, speed, and structured data built in from the first commit.
tw.cap.04.title: Product photography
tw.cap.04.desc: Full catalogs, ready for e-commerce and social, no third parties needed.
tw.cap.05.title: Digital strategy
tw.cap.05.desc: Meta and Google Ads set up with the same brand logic, not as an afterthought.
tw.cta.title: Is your project the next case study
tw.cta.sub: I work with brands in Colombia and remotely worldwide. Tell me what you need — I reply within 24 hours.
orb.hero.sub: A catalog of 3,000+ dental products that took 14 seconds to load and broke on mobile. Customers leaving before seeing a single price. I rebuilt it from scratch — without losing a single product or a single Google ranking — and took PageSpeed from 40 to 97.
orb.metric1: PageSpeed Score — Core Web Vitals passed in one month
orb.metric2: How long it took to load, before
orb.metric4: Responsive — before, it didn't work on mobile
orb.problem.title: A slow website isn't a technical problem. It's a sales problem.
orb.problem.p1: The previous site was built on Divi: it dragged over a megabyte of unused code on every visit, wasn't designed for mobile, and — honestly — looked bad. Flat background, scattered photos, no visual hierarchy. It took 14 seconds to open.
orb.problem.pull: Every second of waiting and every broken screen on a phone was a customer closing the tab.
orb.problem.p2: This was happening on a 3,000+ product catalog where people arrive looking for something specific and in a hurry. For a business selling online, that doesn't show up in the design — it shows up in the sales that never come in.
orb.problem.p3: Divi couldn't be "optimized" — the weight was in the system itself. It had to go. I rebuilt everything in clean code on WordPress, migrated all 3,000+ products without losing existing SEO rankings — one mistake there and months of positioning collapse — and redesigned the visual experience so the site finally conveyed the seriousness of the company.
orb.info.company.label: Company
orb.info.role.label: My role
orb.info.role.value: In charge of all digital · 2+ years
orb.info.category.label: Category
orb.info.stack.label: Stack

orb.info.deliverables.value: Full redesign, performance, technical SEO, responsive
orb.proof.title: From "Failed" to "Passed" in one month.
orb.proof.text: This isn't my opinion — it's Google's own Core Web Vitals report. In one month the site went from "Failed" to "Passed", and PageSpeed climbed from 40 to 97. In practice: 14 seconds of load time turned into a near-instant open.
orb.proof.before.label: Before
orb.proof.after.label: After
orb.showcase.title: Homepage
orb.showcase.text: Before: flat background, no hierarchy, slow. Now: a coherent, organized homepage that looks professional on any screen and loads instantly. A company's credibility is designed too.
orb.gallery1.title: Brand page
orb.gallery1.text: Eighteeth is one of the brands Orbidental distributes. Instead of another generic page, I built a dedicated experience inside the site — proof that this doesn't come from a template, but is designed brand by brand.
orb.gallery2.title: The prototype for the future store
orb.gallery2.text: The current product pages are basic — white background, one photo, unstructured text. The real e-commerce is now in development. To define where it's headed and align the team, I designed this prototype of how the product page will look and work: clear, coherent, and built so customers understand what they're buying and feel confident deciding. This is Orbidental's real next step — and I'm the one building it.
orb.cta.title: Think your site is fast enough
orb.cta.sub: Open it on a phone with mobile data, away from your home network. That's where Orbidental was losing customers without knowing it — and where most stores that "look fine" lose theirs too. If you're not sure, you're probably already losing them. Tell me what you sell and within 24 hours I'll tell you exactly what your current site is costing you.
pd.hero.sub: A premium agency identity built from scratch — in English, for international clients, without a single element borrowed from my own brand.
pd.challenge.title: An international client doesn't ask if I can design — they ask if I can step outside my style.
pd.challenge.p1: My entire portfolio lives in the same visual universe: dark, gold, Fraunces, one voice. That's brand consistency — but for a client looking for a premium English-language marketing agency, that universe doesn't work as a reference. They need to see that I can build a completely different identity with the same level of craft.
pd.challenge.pull: Pulse Digital is that proof: a fictional agency brand built to demonstrate real versatility — not just "I can change colors" but I can switch visual languages entirely and have it feel intentional, not generic.
pd.info.client.label: Client
pd.info.lang.label: Language
pd.result.title: An agency that feels like a consultancy, not another SaaS.
pd.result.text: The final direction was a modern growth/SaaS identity: Poppins typography, violet and lime green as accents on white, and real team photography instead of just text in columns. This is a preview of the site — click to open it in full.
pd.mock.caption: Preview — click anywhere to open the full site in a new tab.
pd.hard.title: Leaving my palette isn't just changing the hex.
pd.hard.text: Anyone can invert colors and call it "another identity". The real challenge was building a system with the same internal logic any real brand has: a typeface with its own personality, a palette where the accent appears with intention, and photography that adds real visual weight — not a generic template with a color swap.
pd.tokens.type.desc: Geometric, rounded — distinct from the serif and Space Grotesk of my own portfolio
pd.tokens.concept.text: Modern growth agency — real photography, bento grid, integrated metrics
pd.solved.label: What I solved
pd.solved.01.title: Composition with real visual weight, not just text in columns
pd.solved.01.desc: Early directions were solid typographically but felt flat: everything stacked in even columns. I researched real references (Awwwards-winning agencies) and the difference was in large-scale photography and asymmetric composition — I applied that to the hero, services, and results bento.
pd.solved.02.title: Photography curation with verified licensing
pd.solved.02.desc: Every image was individually selected and verified for licensing and composition before use — no generic stock banks left unchecked. The result: real team, real dashboard, an active brand feel rather than random stock photos.
pd.solved.03.title: English copy with genuine authority
pd.solved.03.desc: No generic "we help brands grow". The copy was written with a serious agency voice: specific, direct, with honest admissions of limits ("we'll tell you honestly whether we're the right fit") — that builds more trust than any results claim.
pd.cta.title: Does your brand need an identity that doesn't feel generic
pd.cta.sub: Tell me about your project. I reply within 24 hours.
